description_long: This object consists of three stacked stone blocks of varying sizes, each with a roughly hewn, layered appearance typical of sedimentary rock. The stones are irregularly shaped, with flat tops and bottoms, and their edges are slightly rounded, suggesting some degree of weathering. The coloration is predominantly a pale gray with subtle hints of beige and light brown, giving the stones a natural, earthy look. The surfaces are matte and visibly rough, with striations and textural details that mimic real stone. The blocks are arranged vertically, with the largest at the base and the smallest at the top, creating a balanced yet informal stack. There are no visible engravings, markings, or artificial modifications on the stones.
description: A trio of irregular, layered stone blocks stacked vertically, featuring rough, pale gray surfaces with natural weathering and no visible markings.
description_view_0: Three stone blocks stacked vertically, clearly showing the layered structure and rough texture.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the individual shapes and sizes of each block, with the largest on the left and smallest on the right.
description_view_2: Direct view of the stack from above, emphasizing the flat tops and the alignment of the blocks.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, showing the staggered arrangement and the natural, uneven edges of the stones.
attribute_location_context: urban park landscaping or sidewalk barrier
attribute_weathering_pattern: Edges are slightly rounded and surfaces are uneven, indicating mild weathering and exposure to the elements.
